purpose: Demonstrate that weak PM2.5–asthma association is consistent across statistical tests and contrasts with a working positive control — arguing against analytics/data-join failure as the main explanation.

negative_control_shuffled_pm25.note: One random permutation; full null in permutation_test.

verdict_checks.meaning: One row per county FIPS; no duplicate joins.
Pipeline detects strong known county-level association.
Multivariate stack is not broken — weak PM2.5 is not global failure.
Pearson and Spearman both show negligible linear/monotone link.
Cannot reject null of zero association at ~95% bootstrap CI.
|r|=0.057 is below ~0.24 needed for n=67 significance.
Shuffled PM2.5 labels produce similar |r| as often as observed.
PM2.5 correlation does not hinge on one outlier county.
Narrow PM2.5 spread limits how much variance asthma can explain.

interpretation_for_portfolio: If the data pipeline were mis-joined or analytically broken, we would not recover R²≈0.86 on confounders and r≈0.86 for obesity with bootstrap CI excluding zero. PM2.5 instead shows |r|<0.1, CI including zero, permutation p>0.05, and negligible incremental R² — consistent with inherently weak county-level PM2.5 signal after shared health burden, plus limited PM2.5 geographic contrast across Alabama.
epidemiology_context: County ecological studies aggregate heterogeneous individuals; true individual-level effects attenuate.
Asthma prevalence reflects diagnosis, access to care, and genetics — not only air quality.
Alabama counties have similar regional PM2.5 (modelled ~9–12 µg/m³); low exposure contrast reduces statistical power.
Literature: air pollution effects on asthma are often clearer in longitudinal cohort or patient-level studies than crude county maps.

v1_vs_v2_matching.v1_risk: Census-tract asthma (1175 rows) merged with monitor PM2.5 via concat/manual Excel — alignment uncertain.
v1_vs_v2_matching.v2_fix: County FIPS join; same PLACES geolocation for PM2.5 centroid; 67 counties one row each.
v1_vs_v2_matching.implication: v2 weak result is more trustworthy than v1; v1 weak result may have mixed matching noise with true signal.
